message:
type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Adding register_filter/unregister_filter global filter registry to prompts.py

type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Adding filters param to prompt decorator, from_str/from_file, and create_jinja_env() helper

type: INTENT
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: I will implement: prompt(filters=...) decorator param, Prompt.from_str/from_file(filters=...) params, and a new create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper centralizing env creation+builtin filter registration. Will call create_jinja_env from _template_from_str/_template_from_file. If your register_filter/unregister_filter is a global registry, suggest having create_jinja_env also apply globally-registered filters after custom ones, so we don't clobber each other -- let me know your function signatures so I can integrate.

type: ANSWER
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Implemented FILTERS dict + register_filter(name,func)/unregister_filter(name) at top of prompts.py; in _template_from_str and _template_from_file I added 'env.filters.update(FILTERS)' right before/after env creation and before the builtin filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args) are set, so builtins and any locally-passed filters should be applied AFTER env.filters.update(FILTERS) to win precedence over globals. Please add your filters= handling after that update() call so local > global > builtin ordering stays consistent; happy to adjust merge order if you tell me where your create_jinja_env() sets local filters.

type: STATUS
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: My implementation only touches prompt()/from_str/from_file signatures via a filters= kwarg and a new create_jinja_env(loader=None, filters=None) helper; I don't see FILTERS/register_filter in my copy of the file so our sandboxes appear independent. Finalizing my patch as scoped to the filters= param task now.
